title: Gnosis: TV Show Tracker App
description: This app tracks and reviews your favorite TV shows using the TMDB API for detailed information. It centralizes all your TV interests.
challenges: This was my first experience working with an API, and I struggled due to unclear documentation and unfamiliarity with the API’s structure.
solution: I switched to the TMDB API for clearer documentation and better structure, using online guides for support.

title: What's Happening: Event Planner
description: This app offers an intuitive platform for event organization, using Django and Python for the backend, Postgres for the database, and AWS S3 for storage.
challenges: Being my first team project, I faced a steep learning curve with collaborative tools, version control, which made task coordination and conflict management difficult.
solution: I addressed these challenges by focusing on clear communication and regular updates, utilizing GitHub, Slack, and Zoom for effective teamwork and successful project delivery.

title: Inquizitor: Quiz App
description: Inquizitor is a fun little web app for interactive personality quizzes, including Myers-Briggs, Zodiac, and Enneagram. It uses the OpenAI API to generate dynamic questions and explanations based on user responses.
challenges: Complex quiz logic caused loading delays, especially with OpenAI API interactions, impacting user experience.
solution: I optimized API calls and implemented question preloading to reduce load times, enhancing performance and responsiveness for a smoother user experience.

title: Diagnoself: AI Symptom Checker
description: Diagnoself is an AI-powered symptom checker that helps users identify potential health conditions. Users can select symptoms by body part or manually, with recommendations tailored by age and gender using the OpenAI API.
challenges: Implementing clickable, color-changing SVG body parts to display symptoms was a significant challenge.
solution: I used React state to manage the selected body part and applied conditional styling. This allowed for dynamic updates and a more interactive user experience.

title: Cloudy with A Chance of Chaos Probability
description: An interactive system for real-time environmental monitoring, visualizing air quality, temperature, and humidity, with user-friendly displays and timely alerts.
challenges: Limited TypeScript experience made it challenging to manage type errors, data integration, and API calls in a dispersed team setting.
solution: Leaned into TypeScript's strict typing to catch bugs early. Close collaboration and clear communication carried us to 🏆 1st place.

title: Public Domain Finder
description: An innovative tool  designed to help writers locate public domain and fair use material for adaptation and creative reinterpretation. Using the Gutendex API, it lets users search thousands of works, find promising sources, and explore adaptation possibilities while staying within copyright limits.
challenges: Integrating the Gutendex API meant working with large datasets and building efficient search features. Designing an interface that made a massive library feel accessible and relevant to writers required additional care.
solution: The interface provides previews and detailed metadata so writers can evaluate material quickly. Caching was added to improve performance, cut down on API calls, and give users faster access to frequently viewed works.

title: Scriptstreams: A Screenplay Database
description: A comprehensive database/archive of film and television scripts for screenwriters who learn by reading produced screenplays, pilots, and series bibles to study craft and structure.  It is rapidly growing in popularity within the screenwriting community.
challenges: Manage a database of 8000+ scripts while ensuring fast search and retrieval times was a significant challenge.
solution: I implemented efficient querying and indexing strategies to ensure fast search and retrieval times, even with a large dataset.

title: OpenSlug: A Screenwriting Editor
description: A privacy-focused (local-first) screenwriting editor that allows writers to create, edit, and format screenplays. It offers version control, comprehensive import options, industry-standard exports, scene navigation, and mobile support.
challenges: One of the biggest challenges was building an import feature that supported multiple formats while preserving screenplay formatting and structure.
solution: I developed a comprehensive parser that recognized different text formats and converted them into a standardized format compatible with the editor.
